sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: S&P Global: Record sukuk issuance in 2025 - The DESK - The leading source of information for bond traders
feed.summary: S&P Global reported record sukuk issuance of US$264.8 billion in 2025, an increase from $234.9 billion in 2024, driven by strong economic growth, high financing needs in major Islamic finance countries, and declining interest rates. The firm anticipates continued growth in 2026, supported by lower oil prices and strong economic performance in core Islamic finance countries, despite potential volatility risks. Foreign currency denominated issuances also saw significant growth, exceeding US$100 billion in 2025.

feed.title: OMS Energy Technologies Inc.’s Saudi Arabia Subsidiary Earns API Specification 6A Certification, Unlocking New Wellhead Service Opportunities
feed.summary: OMS Energy Technologies Inc.'s Saudi Arabia subsidiary, OMS Oilfield Services Arabia Ltd. (OMS Saudi), has achieved the American Petroleum Institute's (API) Specification 6A certification. This certification is crucial for expanding market share in high-pressure drilling and production, validating OMS Saudi's commitment to quality, safety, and reliability in wellhead and Christmas tree equipment services. The milestone positions OMS Saudi for new high-value contracts and strengthens its regional leadership in the oil and gas industry, contributing to both revenue growth and shareholder value.

feed.title: BlackRock’s Profit Fell, Assets Hit Record $14 Trillion, in Fourth Quarter
feed.summary: BlackRock, the world's largest money manager, reported a profit decline in the fourth quarter of 2025 due to higher expenses, despite its assets under management reaching a record-breaking $14 trillion. The company achieved net income of $1.13 billion, marking a 33% decrease compared to the previous year. However, adjusted earnings per share of $13.16 surpassed analyst expectations.

feed.title: What's Going On With Keurig Dr Pepper Stock Thursday? - Keurig Dr Pepper (NASDAQ:KDP)
feed.summary: Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) and JDE Peet’s N.V. announced a public cash offer by Kodiak BidCo B.V. for JDE Peet’s shares at 31.85 euros per share. Following the acquisition, KDP plans to split into two separate U.S.-listed public companies: one focused on North American beverages and another on global coffee. The offer period is from January 16 to March 27, 2026, and is supported unanimously by JDE Peet’s board.

feed.title: TransUnion and Actable Prove AI Success Starts with Data: Partnership Delivers 10% Lift in Predictive Modeling
feed.summary: TransUnion and Actable have demonstrated that a strong data foundation is crucial for successful AI applications in marketing, reporting a 10% improvement in predictive modeling. Their collaboration focused on a retailer seeking to re-engage customers, where integrating TransUnion’s TruAudience Marketing Solutions data significantly reduced false positives by 19.5%. This success highlights the importance of quality data and identity resolution in maximizing AI's potential for efficient marketing resource allocation and improved ROI, particularly for high-cost campaigns.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Cancels Visits to Labadee Through December
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group has canceled all scheduled calls to Labadee, Haiti, through December 2026, citing ongoing security concerns in Haiti. This decision extends a suspension that began in April 2025, prioritizing the safety of guests and crew. Guests affected by the cancellations will have updated itineraries, with some cruises replacing the Labadee stop with other ports or Royal Caribbean's private island, Perfect Day at CocoCay.

feed.summary: PNC Financial Services Group Inc. (NYSE: PNC) is set to release its Q4 earnings on January 16, with analysts projecting higher earnings per share and revenue compared to the previous year. Several Wall Street analysts have recently updated their ratings and price targets for PNC, generally maintaining positive outlooks. The company also declared a dividend of $1.70 on common stock earlier in January.

feed.title: These Funds Hold the Largest Stakes in Alphabet Stock
feed.summary: Alphabet's stock surge, driven by AI enthusiasm and the release of Gemini 3, has significantly impacted funds heavily invested in the company. The Provident Trust Strategy Fund and Fidelity OTC Portfolio hold the largest stakes, with several ESG-focused funds also appearing on the list of top holders. This performance has propelled Alphabet to become the world's second-largest company, highlighting the strong returns for funds with major allocations to its stock.

feed.title: Clorox stock fights for a fresh narrative as defensive investors circle back to staples
feed.summary: Clorox stock is attempting to regain investor confidence after a challenging year marked by cyber disruption, inflation, and changing consumer behavior. While the stock has seen subdued trading lately, suggesting long-term holders are reassessing its recovery, Wall Street remains divided between cautious realism and selective optimism. The company faces a critical juncture, needing to demonstrate consistent organic growth and margin improvement to move past its current "wait and see" investor sentiment.

feed.title: Rio Tinto and Amazon Web Services collaborate to bring low-carbon Nuton copper to U.S. data centres
feed.summary: Rio Tinto and Amazon Web Services (AWS) have announced a strategic collaboration where AWS will be the first customer for Nuton® Technology's low-carbon copper, using it in components for its U.S. data centers. AWS will also provide cloud-based data and analytics to optimize Nuton's bioleaching technology at the Johnson Camp copper mine. This partnership aims to strengthen domestic supply chains for critical materials and contribute to more sustainable, lower-carbon mining practices.

feed.title: BlackRock Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Earnings
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. (NYSE: BLK) has released its financial results for the fourth quarter of 2025, which are available on its investor relations website. The company's Chairman and CEO Laurence D. Fink, President Robert S. Kapito, and CFO Martin S. Small will host a teleconference and webcast for investors and analysts to discuss the earnings. The webcast will also be archived for replay.

feed.title: Amazon Unleashes Europe-Only Cloud In $9 Billion Expansion Push
feed.summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) launched its European Sovereign Cloud, investing over 7.8 billion euros ($9 billion) in Germany, primarily for its first region in Brandenburg. This new cloud environment is physically and logically separate, designed to meet strict European data sovereignty requirements for governments and businesses handling sensitive data. AWS plans to expand these sovereign cloud services to other EU countries like Belgium, the Netherlands, and Portugal, reinforcing its commitment to advanced cloud and AI infrastructure in the region.

feed.title: Travel + Leisure CEO talks hotel tax hike, brand acquisitions, goals in 2026
feed.summary: Michael Brown, CEO of Travel + Leisure Co., expects Orlando tourism to have a stronger year in 2026 than 2025 based on early booking data. The company, one of Central Florida's largest publicly traded entities with $3.86 billion in revenue in 2025, is closely monitoring a key infrastructure project deemed crucial for the region's continued growth. This outlook also touches on ongoing discussions about a hotel tax hike and potential brand acquisitions.

feed.title: Exclusive | Humble Hershey’s Gets a Marketing Makeover
feed.summary: Hershey's, the 125-year-old chocolate brand, is getting a marketing overhaul with a 20% increase in its advertising budget. This initiative includes its first new advertising campaign in eight years and a focus on attracting younger consumers through product iterations and engagement with TikTok influencers. The goal is to generate year-round sales and freshen up the brand's image.

feed.title: AbbVie’s US$100bn Deal Could Transform US Healthcare Access
feed.summary: AbbVie, along with 15 other major drugmakers, has entered a US$100bn agreement with the Trump administration aimed at significantly reducing medicine costs for Americans and boosting domestic pharmaceutical research, development, and manufacturing. The deal focuses on lower Medicaid pricing and expanded direct-to-patient offerings via the TrumpRx platform, securing AbbVie an exemption from import tariffs and protection from future pricing mandates. This initiative represents a broader industry transformation toward greater affordability and re-shoring of healthcare manufacturing capacity.

feed.title: Smithfield Foods IPO likely to price at $20, below $23-$27 range - reports
feed.summary: Sources indicate that Smithfield Foods' initial public offering is expected to price at $20 per share, which is below the previously announced target range of $23 to $27. This pricing decision comes despite earlier reports suggesting the IPO could raise up to $552 million for the company.
